# reviews — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The reviews feature handles the full lifecycle of actor-to-actor (citizen-to-vport) reviews on the VCSM platform. All write and read orchestration is delegated to the shared `engines/reviews/` engine, which owns the DB schema (`reviews.*`), controllers, DALs, and models. The feature layer at `apps/VCSM/src/features/reviews/` contains only a single setup file that bootstraps the engine at app startup via dependency injection. The real review surfaces are distributed across: the vport profile feature (authenticated review submission, list, stats), the public vportMenu feature (unauthenticated public review read), and the vport dashboard (owner review management). A secondary app-level controller tier sits in `apps/VCSM/src/features/profiles/kinds/vport/controller/review/` and bridges engine shapes to hook contracts.

**Source Path:** `apps/VCSM/src/features/reviews/`
**Engine Path:** `engines/reviews/src/`
**Profile Integration Path:** `apps/VCSM/src/features/profiles/kinds/vport/controller/review/`
**Public Integration Path:** `apps/VCSM/src/features/public/vportMenu/`
**Dashboard Integration Path:** `apps/VCSM/src/features/dashboard/vport/dashboard/cards/reviews/`
**Security Tier:** LOW (PLANNED — no writes active in production yet)
**Feature Status:** PLANNED (engine fully built; app UI integration in progress)

---

## Active Controllers

### Engine Layer (6 controllers in engines/reviews/src/controller/)

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| submitReview.controller.js | Upsert neutral review + optional dimension ratings | isActorOwner(authorActorId) via DI; self-review guard (targetActorId === authorActorId) |
| deleteReview.controller.js | Soft-delete a review; idempotent on already-deleted | isActorOwner(authorActorId) + author_actor_id row match |
| listReviews.controller.js | Paginated list of active reviews for a target actor | None — public read path |
| getMyActiveReview.controller.js | Fetch current author's active neutral review for a target | None — caller must supply valid authorActorId |
| getReviewStats.controller.js | Aggregate stats for a target via DB RPC | None — public read path |
| getReviewFormConfig.controller.js | Load active review dimensions for a target kind/subtype | None — public read path |

### App Profile Integration Layer (3 controllers in apps/.../profiles/kinds/vport/controller/review/)

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| VportReviews.controller.js | Bridges engine controllers to hook contract; adds actor/kind validation, rating key mapping, and notification dispatch | dalReadReviewTargetActor + kind='user' author guard; target must be active vport |
| VportServiceReviews.controller.js | Adds service-tab support; lists vport services for reviews UI; filters reviews by serviceId | Delegates to VportReviews.controller.js |
| vportReviews.mappers.js | Pure mapping functions — engine domain shape to legacy hook shape | N/A |

### App Public Read Layer (2 controllers in apps/.../features/public/vportMenu/controller/)

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| getVportPublicReviews.controller.js | Initial load: summary + first page + dimensions | None — public unauthenticated |
| getVportPublicReviewsPage.controller.js | Pagination continuation | None — public unauthenticated |

---

## Active DALs

### Engine Layer (6 DAL files in engines/reviews/src/dal/)

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| reviews.read.dal.js | reviews.reviews | Cursor-based pagination; explicit column list (REVIEW_COLUMNS); no select(*) |
| reviews.write.dal.js | reviews.reviews | dalInsertReview, dalUpdateReviewBody, dalSoftDeleteReview; all filter by author_actor_id |
| reviews.rpc.dal.js | reviews.reviews (via RPC) | dalRpcUpsertNeutralReview → reviews.upsert_neutral_review(); dalRpcGetTargetOverallStats → reviews.get_target_overall_stats() |
| dimensions.read.dal.js | reviews.review_dimensions | dalListActiveDimensions; filtered by target_kind + target_subtype + is_active |
| dimensionRatings.read.dal.js | reviews.review_dimension_ratings | Batch fetch by reviewIds list (eliminates N+1) |
| dimensionRatings.write.dal.js | reviews.review_dimension_ratings | Upsert on conflict (review_id, dimension_id); dalDeleteDimensionRatingsForReview |

### App Profile Integration Layer (1 DAL in apps/.../profiles/kinds/vport/dal/review/)

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| reviewTarget.read.dal.js | vc.actors | Validates target/author actor: id, kind, vport_id, is_void only |

### App Public Read Layer (3 DAL files in apps/.../features/public/vportMenu/dal/)

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| readPublicVportReviews.dal.js | reviews.public_vport_reviews_v | Public view; paginated with cursor; no auth required |
| readPublicVportReviewSummary.dal.js | reviews.* (summary view) | Aggregate stats for public display |
| readPublicVportReviewDimensions.dal.js | reviews.review_dimensions | Dimensions for public display |

## Active Hooks

| Hook | What it calls | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| useVportReviews.js | ctrlAssertReviewTargetActor, ctrlGetReviewFormConfig, ctrlGetOfficialStats, useVportReviewList, useVportReviewMine | Root orchestrator hook; manages tab state, dimensions, stats, service selection, compose/delete delegation |
| useVportReviewList.js | ctrlListReviews (via VportReviews.controller.js) | Paginated review list state; cursor pagination; loadMore |
| useVportReviewMine.js | ctrlGetMyActiveReview, ctrlSubmitReview (via VportReviews.controller.js) | Current user's review state; submit/delete/edit lifecycle |
| useVportReviewCompose.js | submitReview callback from useVportReviews | Compose form state; dimension ratings map; body; submit handler |
| useVportPublicReviews.js | getVportPublicReviewsController, getVportPublicReviewsPageController | Public unauthenticated review panel state; pagination |

## Engine Dependencies

| Engine | Import Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| reviews engine | @reviews (engines/reviews/index.js → adapters/index.js) | All review read/write logic: submitReview, deleteReview, listReviews, getMyActiveReview, getTargetStats, getReviewFormConfig, models |

The reviews engine is the sole engine dependency. No other engines (identity, booking, chat) are imported by the reviews feature.

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What is imported | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| notifications | publishVcsmNotification via notifications.adapter | reviews → notifications (outbound notification on review_created) |
| profiles/kinds/vport/dal/services | readVportServicesByActor.dal | VportServiceReviews.controller.js → vport DAL (service list for service tab) |
| supabase client | supabaseClient singleton | features/reviews/setup.js → services (DI bootstrap) |

The feature does NOT import from booking, chat, feed, or other domain features. The notifications.adapter call in VportReviews.controller.js is the only outbound cross-feature mutation.

---

## Authorization Pattern

**Engine layer:** Dependency-injected `isActorOwner(authorActorId)` — configured in `apps/VCSM/src/features/reviews/setup.js`. This checks `vc.actor_owners` (not `vc.actors`) with `is_void=false`. If the session RLS blocks the row, the check fails. This is a defense-in-depth client-side pre-guard; real enforcement is DB-level inside the `reviews.upsert_neutral_review()` SECURITY DEFINER function.

**App profile layer:** `ctrlSubmitReview` in `VportReviews.controller.js` applies:
1. Author actor must exist and not be void (`dalReadReviewTargetActor`)
2. Author kind must be `'user'` (citizens only — vports cannot submit reviews)
3. Target must be an active vport with a resolved subtype
4. Self-review prevention (targetActorId === authorActorId)
5. Engine `isActorOwner` check delegates to the DI function above

**Delete path:** Both `author_actor_id` row-match AND `isActorOwner` check must pass. DB-level RLS further enforces author-scoped update access.

**Public read path:** No auth gate — reads from `reviews.public_vport_reviews_v` view (DB-enforced visibility rules).

---

## Module Independence Classification

**DEPENDENT**

The feature at `apps/VCSM/src/features/reviews/` is a thin DI bootstrap only. All substantive logic lives in the engine (`engines/reviews/`) and is distributed across the vport profile feature and the public vportMenu feature. The module cannot be understood or operated independently of those two integration sites. This is an intentional architectural choice — reviews are rendered within profile and public-menu contexts, not as a standalone feature.

---

## Architecture State

**EVOLVING**

The engine layer is fully implemented and well-structured. The app integration layer is substantially built (controllers, hooks, components, screens all present). However, the feature-layer source path (`apps/VCSM/src/features/reviews/`) contains only the setup bootstrap. DR_STRANGE.md classifies this as PLANNED. The DB schema is live but UI integration is not yet complete (THOR_BLOCKED). The dual VportReviewsView.jsx presence (two files at different paths in profiles/screens/) is a structural risk that needs resolution.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **Dual VportReviewsView.jsx** — Two screen files exist at different paths: `apps/VCSM/src/features/profiles/kinds/vport/screens/review/VportReviewsView.jsx` and `apps/VCSM/src/features/profiles/kinds/vport/screens/views/tabs/VportReviewsView.jsx`. This is a potential dead-code or dual-implementation risk. One may be a legacy path.

2. **VportServiceReviews.controller.js service binding caveat** — `ctrlListServiceReviews` falls back to returning ALL reviews when no service FK is present in review rows. Comment in source explicitly flags this as a current schema limitation. If silently relied upon, filtered views will be inaccurate.

3. **Feature source path is near-empty** — Only `setup.js` exists at the canonical feature path. If a future engineer looks for review logic at `apps/VCSM/src/features/reviews/`, they will find nothing and may duplicate work. The architecture is distributed across four locations with no central index.

4. **ctrlDeleteMyReview dual call-style polymorphism** — Accepts both `(reviewId, authorActorId)` and `({ reviewId, requesterActorId })` parameter shapes. This is a code smell indicating the interface was adapted over time and callers may be inconsistent.

5. **isActorOwner pre-check is client-side** — While DB enforcement exists via SECURITY DEFINER RPC, the client-side pre-check pattern is defensive but adds a DB round-trip. The engine CLAUDE.md and setup.js both document this clearly (REV-V-001 fix note) but it must remain consistent across future refactors.

---

## Recommended Handoffs

- **IRONMAN** — Establish ownership record; the feature spans four integration sites with no declared owner.
- **VENOM** — Security audit is UNKNOWN. Submit path, delete path, and the DI isActorOwner pattern all need formal trust boundary analysis.
- **SPIDER-MAN** — No test coverage established. Engine layer controllers are high-value regression targets.
- **DB** — Audit `reviews.upsert_neutral_review()` SECURITY DEFINER function; confirm RLS on `reviews.public_vport_reviews_v`; confirm `reviews.review_dimension_ratings` upsert conflict handling in DB.
- **HAWKEYE** — Map public review routes; confirm endpoint contracts for `/actor/:actorId/reviews`, VportMenu public review screens, and dashboard reviews card.

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

The engine is production-grade and well-structured. App integration is substantially built. The feature is classified PLANNED because the full UI flow is not yet verified in production. All architecture layers are present but distributed; no central feature adapter exists at the reviews/ source path. Two structural risks (dual screen file, service binding fallback) require resolution before THOR clearance.
